file_insights.purpose: Architecture decision record explaining storage model choice
key_sections: When You WOULD Want a Database

patterns_discovered.description: Document lists rejected alternatives (SQLite, Dolt, sled, redb) with specific reasoning for each, not just 'we chose A'
patterns_discovered.value: Prevents recurring proposals and clarifies trade-offs

patterns_discovered.description: Uses table format to compare fs::rename vs SQLite transactions across ACID/operational dimensions
patterns_discovered.value: Makes trade-offs visible at a glance

patterns_discovered.description: Section on scaling considerations addresses when decision might need revisiting (10k→100k+ cards)
patterns_discovered.value: Prevents false permanence while defending current choice

patterns_discovered.description: Cites Maildir, Git, systemd, Kubernetes, Nix as precedent for filesystem-as-storage
patterns_discovered.value: Builds confidence through reference to proven patterns

gotchas_discovered.description: Document ends with 'This is a closed decision' statement with explicit constraint about not proposing databases
gotchas_discovered.implication: Indicates this was a contentious choice that required finality to prevent re-litigation

gotchas_discovered.description: Zero mandatory daemon is listed as core requirement, which rules out entire classes of solutions
gotchas_discovered.implication: This constraint originates from specific deployment/operational philosophy, not just technical preference

gotchas_discovered.description: Event log O_APPEND is only atomic for small writes
gotchas_discovered.implication: Large events might need special handling or size limits

gotchas_discovered.description: Relies on SHA-256 mismatch to trigger JSONL replay, not proactive validation
gotchas_discovered.implication: Corruption is reactive rather than preventive

approach_outcome.deliverable: Comprehensive ADR (Architecture Decision Record) documenting storage design rationale
approach_outcome.completeness: Complete - all major decision dimensions covered

recommendations.recommendation: Add implementation example showing JSONL replay logic on checksum mismatch
recommendations.rationale: Document explains the concept but code readers will need to see actual fallback pattern

recommendations.recommendation: Add section on backup/recovery strategy given append-only JSONL model
recommendations.rationale: JSONL durability depends on filesystem; should document fsync behavior and backup recommendations

recommendations.recommendation: Consider adding timestamp (2026-03-01 per memory) to close statement to track decision age
recommendations.rationale: Helps future reviewers understand how long decision has been stable

recommendations.recommendation: Cross-reference this ADR from CONTRIBUTING.md and architecture docs
recommendations.rationale: New contributors need to find this rationale when they inevitably question the storage choice
